question: Did the model select Option C (A female carrier of a dystrophinopathy) as the final answer?
Did the model correctly identify the visual presence of brown immunostaining along the sarcolemma (cell membrane) of specific muscle fibers?
Did the model correctly describe the staining pattern as a 'mosaic' or a mixture of stained (positive) and unstained (negative) fibers?
Did the model avoid hallucinating that the staining is uniform and continuous around every single fiber (which would indicate a Normal result)?
Did the model avoid hallucinating a complete absence of staining across the entire section (which would indicate Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy)?
Did the model correctly reason that the mosaic pattern of dystrophin expression is consistent with X-chromosome inactivation (Lyonization) in a female carrier?
Did the model logically rule out 'Normal' (Option D) based on the presence of unstained fibers?
Did the model logically rule out 'Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy' (Option B) based on the presence of positively stained fibers?
Did the model differentiate the observed pattern from Becker's Muscular Dystrophy (Option A), noting that Becker's typically shows reduced intensity rather than a distinct all-or-none mosaic pattern?
Did the model explicitly select one of the provided options (A, B, C, or D) without hedging or omitting a final conclusion?
